Rules from jorap-voice:
- `description` = memorable definition in one breath, ≤20 words, NO [[wikilinks]]
- Plain words JoRap would say aloud
- Can use first person when it helps recall, but must answer "what IS this?" not just "what do I do when..."
- Do NOT change key_concept, examples, relationships, cards, or any other field
- Only edit the `description:` line in frontmatter

Good examples:
- "Accountability is naming who owns the result - and what happens when it slips." (from accountability.md - but rewrite if current is situational)
- "David Allen's capture-clarify-organize loop gives me a trusted inbox, next actions, and a weekly review."

Return: file → old description → new description for each file changed.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
All current descriptions have been read.

Beginning to rewrite each description as a memorable definition answering "what is this?" rather than situational advice. Starting with assurance.md, aiming for a crisp, recallable line.

Drafted a definition for composure.md: staying steady in voice and pace when everyone else speeds up. Still working on convert-pain-into-learning.md, trying to avoid situational phrasing.

Finished convert-pain-into-learning.md: failure must produce correction, not rumination. Drafted definitions for build-a-reliable-default.md and control-the-rhythm.md, avoiding situational phrasing.

Drafted a sharper definition for control-the-rhythm.md: whoever sets the pace steers the room, so I take the tempo back when I can. Rewrote own-the-error.md to name the miss clearly so failure becomes information, not blame theater. Rewrote elon-musk.md to treat Elon Musk as the pop-culture shorthand for first-principles thinking, useful as a cite but not gospel. Started first-principles-thinking.md, noting its current description is situational.

Finished first-principles-thinking.md: rebuilding from what I actually believe, not someone else's quote. Drafted graph-view-analytics.md to read the link map for orphans, hubs, and forgotten gaps. Drafted growth-mindset.md to treat ability as trainable, mistakes as data, not a fixed identity.

Drafted gtd-vs-para.md: GTD and PARA are cousins, not rivals. Rewrote leadership.md to own direction, pace, and care when people look to you; title optional. Rewrote mental-models.md to thinking shortcuts that survive real decisions. Started mobile-capture-workflows.md but noted it currently mixes problem and solution.

Drafted mobile-capture-workflows.md: saving the spark in one tap before scrolling away. Rewrote notice-the-good.md to name the specific helpful move so people know what to repeat. Tightened pareto-principle.md to say most results come from a small slice of the work. Started set-calm-boundaries.md but noted its current description is action-oriented.

Finished set-calm-boundaries.md: a calm boundary is one clear limit on behavior, named in a steady voice, not a character attack. Rewrote slow-productivity.md to mean fewer active projects so work finishes instead of piling as guilt. Verified that accountability.md already contains the exact line the user wants: naming who owns the result and what happens when it slips. Started compounding.md but noted its current description is somewhat poetic and situational.

Drafted compounding.md: small gains repeated until the curve bends. Checked word counts against the ≤20-word target. Assurance, composure, and convert-pain are all under 15 words. Still counting the remaining files.
</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

All 20 files updated. Only `description:` changed.

| File | Old | New |
|------|-----|-----|
| `assurance.md` | When doubt shows up, I rest on what Christ already finished, not on how clean my week looked. | Assurance is resting on Christ's finished work when doubt hits - not my week's obedience score. |
| `composure.md` | When the room spikes, I slow my tone so people read calm instead of panic. | Composure is steady voice and pace when the room isn't - people read calm as competence. |
| `convert-pain-into-learning.md` | I only gain from a failure if it changes how I act the next time the situation shows up. | Convert pain into learning means failure must produce correction, not rumination without change. |
| `build-a-reliable-default.md` | When I'm tired, I fall back on what I've rehearsed most - so that fallback has to be simple. | A reliable default is the simple rehearsed move I run when thinking is thin. |
| `control-the-rhythm.md` | I set pace in hard conversations before adrenaline picks it for me. | Control the rhythm is taking back pace before urgency, conflict, or someone else sets it. |
| `own-the-error.md` | When I miss, I say I missed - blame-shifting turns a fixable bug into a long argument. | Owning the error is naming the miss clearly so failure becomes information, not blame theater. |
| `elon-musk.md` | When someone needs a pop-culture name for strip-to-basics thinking, Musk is the cite I reach for, not the gospel. | Elon Musk is the pop-culture shorthand for first-principles thinking - useful cite, not gospel. |
| `first-principles-thinking.md` | When a note is just someone else's quote, I add a section on what I actually believe from scratch. | First principles thinking is rebuilding from what I actually believe, not someone else's quote. |
| `graph-view-analytics.md` | I use the link graph to spot orphans and over-connected hubs, not because it looks sci-fi pretty. | Graph view analytics reads my link map for orphans, over-connected hubs, and gaps I'd forgotten. |
| `growth-mindset.md` | I treat a rough outing as data about what to practice next, not proof I'm stuck with a fixed label. | Growth mindset treats ability as trainable - mistakes are data, not proof of a fixed label. |
| `gtd-vs-para.md` | I run tasks through GTD and notes through PARA as cousins that share capture, not rival systems that double admin. | GTD vs PARA runs tasks in one system and notes in another - cousins, not rivals. |
| `leadership.md` | When the room goes quiet and everyone looks at me, I own what happens next - title optional. | Leadership is owning direction, pace, and care when people look to you - title optional. |
| `mental-models.md` | I keep a short list of thinking shortcuts that survived real decisions - not every model on Wikipedia. | Mental models are thinking shortcuts I keep when they survive real decisions. |
| `mobile-capture-workflows.md` | Most sparks die on mobile because capture takes twelve taps. I reduced mine to share sheet → inbox. | Mobile capture is one tap to save the spark before I scroll away - share sheet to inbox. |
| `notice-the-good.md` | I name the specific helpful move out loud so people know what to repeat. | Notice the good means naming the specific helpful move so people know what to repeat. |
| `pareto-principle.md` | Most results come from a small slice of work - find the slice, protect it, cut the rest. | The Pareto principle is the 80/20 rule - most results come from a small slice of effort. |
| `set-calm-boundaries.md` | I name one clear limit on behavior in a steady voice - not a character attack. | A calm boundary is one clear limit on behavior, named steady - not a character attack. |
| `slow-productivity.md` | I run fewer active projects so notes become finished work, not a guilt backlog. | Slow productivity is fewer active projects so work finishes deep instead of piling as guilt. |
| `accountability.md` | I name one owner for the outcome before the group chat turns into someone should fix it. | Accountability is naming who owns the result - and what happens when it slips. |
| `compounding.md` | I keep doing the small right thing until the curve finally bends - flat for a long time, then not. | Compounding is small gains stacked over time - the curve stays flat until it suddenly bends. |
